deep_dive.title: Data-Driven Equation Discovery for Nonlinear Liquid Film Flows
deep_dive.abstract: Over the past decade data-driven equation discovery emerged as a powerful alternative to first principles-based methodologies traditionally used in mathematical modelling cycles. The approach provides a promising path towards deep, physical insight into systems that have previously evaded rigorous mathematical derivation procedures, often due to intractable complexity. The strengths of such techniques have been successfully established for many problem classes described by systems of ordinary differential equations and continue to be extended, with their reach into partial differential equation systems gaining momentum, though comparatively nascent. Herein we tackle such a frontier: elucidating the dynamics of liquid film flows, a problem space providing a rich backdrop in terms of asymptotic analytical building blocks. By leveraging expert knowledge and the ability to carefully curate data, we establish a best-case scenario for identifying the underlying governing equations. Even here, multi-collinearity, stemming from the choice of monomial basis functions in our multi-scale flow configuration, introduces complex identifiability issues. Early-time transients compound this further, as the most dynamically rich behaviour carries the largest residuals in training data. Pinpointing such vulnerabilities allows us to better define the boundaries of current discovery techniques and paves the way for the next generation of more resilient, numerically stable algorithms.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Data-driven equation discovery for liquid film flows
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 液膜流动的数据驱动方程发现
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 液膜流动的数据驱动方程发现
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 液膜流动的数据驱动方程发现
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Pioneers data-driven equation discovery for liquid film flows, using sparse regression to identify nonlinear PDEs from simulation data, filling a gap in accurate falling film dynamics models for heat transfer and CFD.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该文将数据驱动方程发现方法应用于液体薄膜流动，从数值模拟数据中通过稀疏回归辨析支配非线性偏微分方程。传统液膜流动建模常依赖简化假设，该研究填补了复杂工况下精确模型推导的空白。结果表明，数据驱动方法能有效揭示液膜动力学方程，为降膜蒸发、换热等过程的CFD模拟提供更可靠的模型基础，推动了多相流建模的前沿发展。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该文将数据驱动方程发现方法应用于液体薄膜流动，从数值模拟数据中通过稀疏回归辨析支配非线性偏微分方程。传统液膜流动建模常依赖简化假设，该研究填补了复杂工况下精确模型推导的空白。结果表明，数据驱动方法能有效揭示液膜动力学方程，为降膜蒸发、换热等过程的CFD模拟提供更可靠的模型基础，推动了多相流建模的前沿发展。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: Papers central to 请帮我查找与传热管垂直降膜换热、CFD模拟相关的论文, especially work that connects or combines: heat transfer in falling film; falling film on vertical tube; two-phase flow simulation; liquid film thickness measurement; multiphase flow CFD; heat and mass transfer in falling film; CFD modeling of falling film heat transfer in vertical tubes; Numerical simulation of heat transfer in vertical falling film heat exchangers; Heat transfer characteristics of falling film over vertical tubes using computational fluid dynamics; Falling film evaporation and heat transfer in vertical tubes CFD.

quick_skim.title: Translation dynamics of evaporating sessile binary-mixture droplet populations
quick_skim.abstract: The translation dynamics of two binary mixture droplets is investigated theoretically and is corroborated with experiments. The proposed model accounts for the effects of Marangoni stresses generated by evaporative cooling and concentration gradients, as well as vapour diffusion, for both components of the binary mixture. We consider thin droplets, allowing us to use the lubrication theory to derive the evolution equation for the droplet profiles. We numerically solve the evolution equations using the finite element method and examine various cases of pure and binary droplet pairs exhibiting translational behaviours like attraction, repulsion, and 'chasing'. The results show that the combined effect of solutal Marangoni, capillary effect, and thermal Marangoni determines the movement of the droplets. The non-uniform evaporation generated from 'vapour shielding' creates such effects. We observe that for droplets with the same initial composition, solutal Marangoni and capillary forces induce droplet attraction, while thermal Marangoni effects drive their repulsion. For droplets with different initial compositions, the drop with a higher concentration of the more volatile component pushes, or `chases', the drop with a lower initial concentration of this component, completely driven by the solutal Marangoni. We carried out experiments involving water-morpholine binary mixture droplets to validate the results predicted by our model.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Studies evaporating thin binary-mixture droplets with Marangoni stresses from evaporative cooling and concentration gradients, relevant to heat and mass transfer in thin liquid films.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 研究蒸发薄二元混合液滴，考虑蒸发冷却和浓度梯度引起的Marangoni应力，与薄液膜传热传质相关。
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 研究蒸发薄二元混合液滴，考虑蒸发冷却和浓度梯度引起的Marangoni应力，与薄液膜传热传质相关。
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 研究蒸发薄二元混合液滴，考虑蒸发冷却和浓度梯度引起的Marangoni应力，与薄液膜传热传质相关。
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Investigates translation dynamics of two thin evaporating binary-mixture droplets, incorporating Marangoni effects from thermal and solutal gradients. Using lubrication theory and finite element simulations, it predicts attraction, repulsion, and chasing behaviors, providing insight into coupled heat and mass transfer in thin films.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 研究两个薄二元混合液滴的蒸发平动动力学，考虑热和溶质毛细效应。采用润滑理论推导演化方程，通过有限元数值模拟揭示了液滴对之间的吸引、排斥和追逐行为。该工作为理解降膜蒸发中的多物理场耦合传输提供了微观机理参考。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 研究两个薄二元混合液滴的蒸发平动动力学，考虑热和溶质毛细效应。采用润滑理论推导演化方程，通过有限元数值模拟揭示了液滴对之间的吸引、排斥和追逐行为。该工作为理解降膜蒸发中的多物理场耦合传输提供了微观机理参考。
